parts.instruction: You will hear people talking in eight different situations. For questions 1–8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).

questions.prompt: You hear a message on a telephone answering machine. Why is the speaker calling?

options.label: to confirm some arrangements
to issue an invitation
to persuade someone to do something

questions.prompt: You hear two people talking about a water-sports centre. The man says the centre should

options.label: pay more attention to safety
offer activities for small children
provide all the equipment needed

questions.prompt: You hear a professional tennis player talking about her career. What annoys her most about interviewers?

options.label: their belief that she leads a glamorous life
their assumption that she's motivated by money
their tendency to disturb her while she's travelling

questions.prompt: You hear a poet talking about his work. What is he doing?

options.label: giving his reasons for starting to visit schools
justifying the childlike nature of some of his recent poems
explaining that his poems appeal to people of different ages

questions.prompt: You hear two people talking about a programme they saw on TV. The woman thinks the programme was

options.label: irritating
sad
uninformative

questions.prompt: You hear two people talking about an ice-hockey game they've just seen. How does the girl feel about it?

options.label: pleased to have had the experience
relieved that she'd dressed appropriately
impressed by the performance of the team

questions.prompt: You overhear two friends talking about a restaurant. What do they both like about it?

options.label: the presentation of the food
the atmosphere of the place
the originality of the cooking

questions.prompt: You hear a man talking on the radio. What type of information is he giving?

options.label: a travel announcement
a weather forecast
an accident report

parts.instruction:
You will hear a woman called Angela Thomas, who works for a wildlife organisation, talking about the spectacled bear.
For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

questions.prompt: Angela says that it was the … of the spectacled bear that first interested her.
Angela mentions that the bear's markings can be found on its … as well as its eyes and cheeks.
Angela is pleased by evidence that spectacled bears have been seen in … areas of Argentina.
Angela says the bears usually live in …, though they can also be found in other places.
Spectacled bears behave differently from other types of bear during …, which Angela finds surprising.
Angela is upset that … are the biggest danger to spectacled bears.
Angela says that spectacled bears usually eat … and tree bark.
Bears climb trees and make a …, which fascinated Angela.
When bears eat meat, they much prefer … although they do eat other creatures.
One man has produced an amusing … about the time he spent studying the bears.

parts.instruction: You will hear five short extracts in which people are talking about their visit to a city. For questions 19–23, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker liked most about the city they visited. Use the letters only once. There are three extra letters which you do not need to use.

questions.prompt: Speaker 1

options.label: the efficiency of the public transport system
the natural beauty of the scenery
the variety of goods in the markets
the style of the architecture
the well-designed plan of the city
the helpfulness of the people
the range of leisure opportunities
the standard of the accommodation

questions.prompt: Speaker 2

options.label: the efficiency of the public transport system
the natural beauty of the scenery
the variety of goods in the markets
the style of the architecture
the well-designed plan of the city
the helpfulness of the people
the range of leisure opportunities
the standard of the accommodation

options.label: the efficiency of the public transport system

options.label: the efficiency of the public transport system

the style of the architecture

questions.prompt: Speaker 5

options.label: the efficiency of the public transport system

parts.instruction: You will hear part of a radio interview with a woman called Rachel Reed, who works in a commercial art gallery. For questions 24–30, choose the best answer (A, B or C).

questions.prompt: What does Rachel say about her job title?

options.label: It makes her feel more important than she is.
It gives people the wrong idea about her work.
It is appropriate for most of the work she does.

questions.prompt: What is the most common reason for the gallery not exhibiting an artist's work?

options.label: The subject matter is unsuitable.
It is not of a high enough quality.
The gallery manager doesn't like it.

questions.prompt: When can phone calls from artists be difficult for Rachel?

options.label: when their work doesn't sell
when they don't receive payments
when their work is not accepted

questions.prompt: Why does Rachel include a commentary in the catalogue?

options.label: It gives background information about the artist.
It encourages people to buy paintings over the phone.
It tells people what experts think of the work.

questions.prompt: What does Rachel say about administrative work?

options.label: She is able to leave a lot of it to others.
She would like to have an assistant to help with it.
She finds it hard to get it all organised.

questions.prompt: What is Rachel's role in the service the gallery offers to large companies?

options.label: making initial contacts
responding to enquiries
promoting a certain type of art

questions.prompt: What does Rachel find most enjoyable about her job?

options.label: meeting interesting people
the fact that it's unpredictable
being close to works of art
